content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

July 22, 1977

John Todd
City of Lakewood
4909 Lakewood Boulevard
Suite 300
Lakewood, California 90712

Dear Mr. Todd:

Enclosed is a copy of SB 338, which was signed by
the Governor on July 7, 1977, as Chapter 230.

This bill clarifies the law as to the civil
prosecutor for local matters under the Political Reform
Act.

Please call on me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:nc

Enclosure

City Attorney
John Sanford Todd                    CITY OF LAKEWOOD
                                    4909 LAKEWOOD BOULEVARD
                                    SUITE 300
                                    LAKEWOOD, CALIFORNIA
                                    (213) 531-1805

                                                            April 5, 1977


Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

                    Re: Todd, as City Attorney of the City of Lakewood,
                        vs. Donald R. Plunkett

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

     Prior to the March, 1976 municipal election I filed suit as
City Attorney of the City of Lakewood against a candidate Donald
R. Plunkett for failure to disclose both his campaign expenditures
and economic interest. The suit is in four counts and filed in
the Southeast Branch of the Los Angeles County Superior Court.
I sought both injunctive relief and damages. The court at a hear-
ing on the preliminary injunction granted only partial injunctive
relief.

     After Mr. Plunkett was elected to the Lakewood City Council in
March of 1976 I requested the California Attorney General to
take over the case and continue the civil action on behalf of the
City of Lakewood. The Attorney General, after reviewing the
matter, concluded that I as City Attorney was the proper person
to continue with the suit as civil prosecutor, and that there
was no conflict of interest or other condition existing prevent-
ing me from carrying out the litigation.

     Around the same time the Attorney General issued his opinion the
Political Reform Act was amended to delete "city attorney" as
civil prosecutor, and bestowing that function on the district
attorney. I therefore requested the Los Angeles County District
Attorney take over the prosecution of this civil action.
Please find enclosed photocopy of response I received from the
Los Angeles County District Attorney dated March 4, 1977. You
will note that the District Attorney concluded that Senate Bill
1401, an urgency bill, was not properly chaptered as Chapter 594,

Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
April 5, 1977
Page Two


and is of no effect. Apparently they have taken the position that
the earlier passage of SB 1401 (Chapter 594) made §6.5 of SB1356
(Chapter 1161) inapplicable by reason of the provisions of §9.
Apparently the argument is:

1.    SB 1401 amending §91001 (and deleting the City Attorney as
     civil prosecutor) became effective as an urgency statute
     on August 27, 1976 and was chaptered as 594.

2.    SB 1356, not having an urgency clause, became effective
     January 1, 1977, chaptered as 1161. As a result §9 of
     SB 1356 was ineffective, which provided that §6.5 of that
     Act became operative only if that bill and SB 1401 are
     both chaptered and become effective January 1, 1977, and
     both amend §91001.

3.    Apparently this argument would then contend that inasmuch
     as both bills did not become effective January 1, 1977
     §6.5 of SB 1356 amending §91001 to contain the same
     language of SB 1401 was ineffective and not adopted, and
     rather §6 of SB1356 amending §91001 was effective. This
     latter section fails to delete the City Attorney as
     civil prosecutor.

Before responding to the District Attorney I would appreciate
any comments your office may have and, if you agree with the
District Attorney, whether there is any urgency cleanup legis-
lation to remedy this defect. Furthermore I disagree with the
interpretation of the District Attorney of the effect of §9
of SB 1356. Obviously the purpose of §9 of SB 1356 was to
incorporate in that bill similar provisions as those set forth
in SB 1401. Inasmuch as there was another bill pending on the
same subject matter §9 provided that §6.5 would be inoperative
and would become effective only if both SB 1401 and SB 1356
are chaptered and become effective January 1, 1977. The effective
date of January 1, 1977 was used in §9 inasmuch as SB 1356
not having an urgency clause would not become sooner effective.
The language was not intended to mean that both bills had to be-
come effective January 1, 1977 and rather was intended to mean
that §6.5 of SB 1356 would only be effective if SB 1401 is also
effective. It appears to me that §9 in fact states this stating
it is the intent of the legislature that if SB 1356 is chaptered
after SB 1401 §6.5 shall become operative and §6 shall not be-
come operative. Such happened in this case and §6 should not be
operative and §6.5 should be operative, contrary to County Coun-
sel's opinion.

Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
April 5, 1977
Page Three

Please advise.
Sincerely;

[signature]

JST:ft/gc

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
SPECIAL INVESTIGATIONS DIVISION
18600 CRIMINAL COURTS BUILDING
210 WEST TEMPLE STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012
974-2688

JOSEPH P BUSCH DISTRICT ATTORNEY
JOHN E HOWARD, CHIEF DEPUTY DISTRICT ATTORNEY
GORDON JACOBSON, ASSISTANT DISTRICT ATTORNEY


March 4, 1977


Mr. John Sanford Todd
City Attorney
City of Lakewood
4909 Lakewood Boulevard
Suite 300
Lakewood, California  90712

Dear Mr. Todd:

We have corresponded with each other on the subject of which
of our agencies should properly serve as civil prosecutor in
matters arising under the Political Reform Act of 1974 which
involve candidates for office in the City of Lakewood.  More
specifically, you have inquired of our opinion as to whether
you should proceed with the prosecution of the pending case
of Todd v. Plunkett.

As you point out in your letter of October 25, 1976, this
action was filed under the provisions of the PRA then existing
wherein the City Attorney of a general law city was the civil
prosecutor.  The subsequent passage of SB 1401 as an emergency
measure in the 1976 Legislative Session seemed to relieve City
Attorneys of general law cities from this responsibility as to
future cases.  Prosecutive responsibility with respect to
cases filed by City Attorneys before the effective date of
SB 1401 presented a further problem.

We apologize for our delay in responding to you with a
definitive answer but felt that resolution of the issue
depended upon clarification of the effect of chaptering.

You will recall that the contemporaneous passage of Section 9
of Stats. 1976, c. 1161 contained language which appeared to
raise a double joinder problem.

It now appears clear that the effect of the chaptering of
these bills has been such that Senate Bill 1401 is not the law

Mr. John Sanford Todd
Page Two
March 4, 1977

of the State of California. Government Code Section 91001
as it appears in the 1976 edition of West's California Codes
appears correctly to reflect the state of the law. Insofar
as prosecutive responsibility is concerned, the Political
Reform Act remains unchanged from its original form.

We conclude that you continue to be the civil prosecutor in
matters arising under the Political Reform Act of 1974 which
involve public officers of the City of Lakewood and candidates for such offices. We have conferred with deputies of
the Attorney General on this question and they concur in our
opinion.

If there is any further issue which requires our mutual
discussion, please call me at the number on this letterhead
or Deputy District Attorney Robert N. Sweet at 974-3906.

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
District Attorney

By [illegible]
BRUCE CAMPBELL, Head
Special Investigations Division


mw

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 17, 1977


John Todd
City of Lakewood
4909 Lakewood Boulevard
Lakewood, California 90712

Dear Mr. Todd:

     Enclosed is a copy of SB 338 which will clarify the
question of who is designated to bring local actions under
the Political Reform Act.

     I will let you know when it is signed by the Governor.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

Enclosure


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (714) 278-1100          ENFORCEMENT (916)


91/301

City Attorney
John Sanford Todd

CITY OF LAKEWOOD

4909 LAKEWOOD BOULEVARD

SUITE 300

LAKEWOOD, CALIFORNIA

(213) 531-1805

April 5, 1977

Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Todd, as City Attorney of the City of Lakewood,
vs. Donald R. Plunkett

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

and is of no effect. Apparently they have taken the position that

latter section fails to delete the City Attorney as

Before responding to the District Attorney I would appreciate
any comments your office may have and, if you agree with the
District Attorney, whether there is any urgency cleanup legis-
lation to remedy this defect. Furthermore I disagree in the
interpretation of the District Attorney of the effect of §9
of SB 1356. Obviously the purpose of §9 of SB 1356 was to
incorporate in that bill similar provisions as those set forth
in SB 1401. Inasmuch as there was another bill pending on the
same subject matter §9 provided that §6.5 would be inoperative
and would become effective only if both SB 1401 and SB 1356
are chaptered and become effective January 1, 1977. The effective
date of January 1, 1977 was used in §9 inasmuch as SB 1356
not having an urgency clause would not become soon effective.
The language was not intended to mean that both bills had to be-
come effective January 1, 1977 and rather was intended to mean
that §6.5 of SB 1356 would only be effective if SB 1401 is also
effective. It appears to me that § 9 in fact states this stating

come operative. Such happened in this case and §6 should not be
operative and §6.5 should be operative, contrary to County Coun-
sel's opinion.

Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
April 5, 1977
Page Three

Please advise.
                                                Sincerely;

                                                [illegible]

RECD MAR -8 1977

Mr. John Sanford Todd
Page Two
March 4, 1977

of the State of California. Government Code Section 91001

Very truly yours,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
District Attorney

By [illegible]

BRUCE CAMPBELL, Head
Special Investigations Division

sections.analysis:
, please call me at the number on this letterhead 
or Deputy District Attorney Robert N. Sweet at 974-3906.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Lakewood is seeking FPPC guidance on whether legislative chaptering of Senate Bills 1401 and 1356 resolves conflicting provisions regarding who should serve as civil prosecutor under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence to FPPC, not an FPPC advice response. Document contains both the original request letter from Todd to FPPC (dated April 5, 1977) and the prior District Attorney opinion letter he references (dated March 4, 1977). The document quality is poor with significant OCR artifacts in reproduction. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections are present as this is a request for guidance rather than a responsive advice letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The City Attorney of Lakewood is seeking FPPC guidance on whether legislative chaptering of Senate Bills 1401 and 1356 resolves conflicting provisions regarding who should serve as civil prosecutor under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: City Attorney John Sanford Todd requests FPPC guidance regarding conflicting provisions in Senate Bills 1401 and 1356 concerning civil prosecutor responsibilities under the Political Reform Act, following his litigation against candidate Donald R. Plunkett and the Los Angeles District Attorney's opinion that the City Attorney remains the proper civil prosecutor.
